question: Which statement best defines female infertility?

options.text: Inability to conceive after 12 months of trying, or 6 months if over 35
options.explanation: Female infertility is defined as not becoming pregnant after at least one year of trying, or six months if older than 35.

options.text: Any miscarriage after the first trimester
options.explanation: Recurrent miscarriage can be associated with infertility, but it is not the definition itself.

options.text: Failure to ovulate during one menstrual cycle
options.explanation: Missing ovulation in one cycle is common and does not by itself define infertility.

options.text: Inability to conceive within 3 months of trying
options.explanation: Three months is too short a time to meet the definition of infertility.

options.text: Fluid-filled sacs on the ovary, often harmless and self-resolving
options.explanation: Ovarian cysts are fluid-filled sacs that often cause no harm and may go away on their own.

options.text: Solid ovarian tumors that always require immediate surgery
options.explanation: Most ovarian cysts are not solid tumors and do not always need surgery.

options.text: Infections of the ovary causing fever and pus
options.explanation: Ovarian cysts are not infections; they are fluid-filled sacs.

options.text: Blood clots within the ovary from trauma
options.explanation: Ovarian cysts are not blood clots and are not caused by trauma.

options.text: Inability to conceive after one year of unprotected intercourse
options.explanation: Infertility is defined as not becoming pregnant after one year of trying.

options.text: Inability to conceive after six months of unprotected intercourse
options.explanation: Six months is too short for the standard definition of infertility.

options.text: Any pregnancy loss before 20 weeks
options.explanation: Pregnancy loss alone does not define infertility unless recurrent and associated with inability to carry a pregnancy.

options.text: Failure to ovulate in a single menstrual cycle
options.explanation: Failure to ovulate once does not by itself meet the definition of infertility.

question: Which description best defines abnormal vaginal bleeding?

options.text: Bleeding only during normal monthly menstruation
options.explanation: Normal monthly menstruation is expected bleeding, not abnormal vaginal bleeding.

options.text: Bleeding between periods, prolonged bleeding, or bleeding before puberty/after menopause
options.explanation: Abnormal vaginal bleeding includes bleeding outside normal menstruation, such as between periods, prolonged bleeding, or bleeding at inappropriate ages.

options.text: Any vaginal discharge with pelvic pain
options.explanation: Vaginal discharge with pelvic pain is not the definition of vaginal bleeding.

options.text: Bleeding only after sexual intercourse
options.explanation: Bleeding after intercourse can occur, but it is not the general definition of abnormal vaginal bleeding.

question: Which condition most commonly causes vaginal disease symptoms such as itching, burning, pain, abnormal bleeding, and discharge?

options.text: Vaginitis
options.explanation: Vaginitis is the most common cause of vaginal symptoms and often presents with smelly discharge.

options.text: Cervicitis
options.explanation: Cervicitis affects the cervix rather than being the most common cause of vaginal symptoms.

options.text: Endometriosis
options.explanation: Endometriosis causes pelvic pain and infertility, not typical vaginal discharge or itching.

options.text: Ovarian cysts
options.explanation: Ovarian cysts usually cause pelvic pain or pressure, not primary vaginal symptoms.

question: Which management approach is appropriate for Mayer-Rokitansky-Küster-Hauser syndrome?

options.text: Vaginal dilation, surgery, and genetic counseling
options.explanation: MRKH syndrome is managed with vaginal dilation or reconstructive surgery and supportive genetic counseling.

options.text: Antibiotic therapy and pelvic radiation
options.explanation: Antibiotics and radiation do not treat congenital vaginal and uterine agenesis.

options.text: Insulin therapy and ovarian suppression
options.explanation: Insulin and ovarian suppression are not treatments for this congenital Müllerian anomaly.

options.text: Chemotherapy and hysterectomy
options.explanation: Chemotherapy is inappropriate, and hysterectomy is not a treatment for absent uterus.
